description_long: This is a freestanding, rectangular postbox with a rounded, arched top, commonly found on city streets in the United States. The exterior is painted a deep blue with a slightly worn, weathered finish, and a darker vertical stripe runs down the center of the front and top. The postbox stands on four short legs, elevating it slightly above the ground. The mail slot is located near the top of the front face, integrated into the curved section, and is covered by a flush panel. There are no visible logos, collection times panels, or other markings on the exterior. The overall construction appears to be robust, likely made of metal for durability and security.
description: A blue, arched-top metal postbox with a front mail slot and a weathered finish, standing on four short legs. It features a simple, utilitarian design with no visible logos or information panels.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the arched top, front mail slot, and the vertical dark stripe; the front face is most visible here.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the solid back panel and the curvature of the top; no access panel or markings are visible.
description_view_2: Rear-right view showing the plain back and side, with the arched top and no visible openings or features.
description_view_3: Front-right view displaying the side and partial front, with the mail slot and dark stripe partially visible.
attribute_mail_slot_location: centered on the front face near the top, integrated into the curved section
attribute_posting_aperture_design: horizontal rectangular slot flush with the front face
